exampleSentences: Giá của chiếc áo này rất hợp lí so với chất lượng.
Anh nghĩ phương án đó là hợp lí cho cả hai bên.
Em nên chọn giờ hợp lí để tránh tắc đường khi đi làm.
notes:
hợp lí = reasonable, makes sense, fair in a practical way. It’s used when something feels logical or balanced.

It’s an adjective, so it describes things like prices, plans, decisions, requests, or explanations. You often use it after nouns or after “thấy / nghe / có vẻ”.

Strong opposite: vô lí (illogical, unreasonable). A stronger, more emotional version is hợp tình hợp lí (reasonable and fair both logically and emotionally).
notes_md:
**hợp lí** = reasonable, makes sense, fair in a practical way. It’s used when something feels logical or balanced.

It’s an adjective, so it describes things like prices, plans, decisions, requests, or explanations. You often use it after nouns or after “thấy / nghe / có vẻ”.


Strong opposite: **vô lí** (illogical, unreasonable). A stronger, more emotional version is **hợp tình hợp lí** (reasonable and fair both logically and emotionally).
exampleSentencesTranslated: The price of this shirt is very reasonable compared to the quality.
I think that solution is reasonable for both sides.
You should choose a reasonable time to avoid traffic jams when going to work.
